stimulus: The Alpine Climate Observatory in Switzerland is known for translating glacier data into musical compositions. One such project pairs recordings of cracking ice with measurements of glacial retreat captured over a decade. As the rate of retreat accelerates, the pitch of the accompanying melody rises; ______blank audiences can "hear" the pace at which the glacier is shrinking.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. "Consequently" logically signals that the information in this clause—that audiences can "hear" the pace at which the glacier is shrinking—is a result of the previous information about the melody's pitch rising as the rate of retreat accelerates.

Choice A is incorrect because "by contrast" illogically signals that the audience's ability to hear the pace is being compared as a contrast with the melody rising; instead, it is a result of that rise. Choice B is incorrect because "moreover" illogically signals that the information merely adds to the previous claim, rather than identifying it as a consequence of the melody's design. Choice D is incorrect because "for example" illogically signals that the audience's experience exemplifies the rising melody, when the rising melody was created precisely for the purpose of producing that experience.
